marketSize_JOD.note: 70% of population under 30, 40%+ youth unemployment drives skills demand. VTC serves 20K/year publicly — private gap is large.
marketSize_JOD.aiPromptGuidance: For Tawjihi prep centers use tawjihiPrepAmman. For general K-12 tutoring use privateTutoringAmman. For online learning use edtechDigitalJordan. For corporate training use corporateTrainingJordan.

online_edtech.name: Abwaab
online_edtech.type: Online learning platform for secondary students
online_edtech.targetSegment: Secondary school students K-12 including Tawjihi
online_edtech.mainStrength: Investor-backed, expert tutors, self-paced learning, strong brand recognition

online_edtech.name: Edraak
online_edtech.targetSegment: University students and adults, Arabic learners
online_edtech.mainStrength: Free/subsidized courses, Queen Rania brand, massive Arabic content library

online_edtech.name: Ustazi
online_edtech.mainStrength: On-demand booking, rating system, lower cost than physical centers

physical_centers.name: Manara Educational Center
physical_centers.targetSegment: Tawjihi students and secondary school pupils
physical_centers.mainStrength: Established track record with Tawjihi pass rates, parent word-of-mouth

physical_centers.name: A+ Private Tutoring
physical_centers.mainStrength: Specialized curriculum, established reputation among expat families

corporate_training.name: Salalem
corporate_training.mainStrength: B2B focus, employee training management, established client base

marketContext.marketGap: Private digital skills, English, coding — high demand, underserved by public institutions.
marketContext.insight: 60% of early-stage entrepreneurs aged 18-34. Young, underemployed, skills-hungry market. Tawjihi prep is recession-proof demand.

startupCost_JOD.legalNote: Physical training centers must register with Ministry of Education. TVET governed by VTC Law (1985) + 2019 amendments. Registration can take 4-8 weeks.

physical_center_1classroom.note: One classroom, one instructor. Suitable for starting small.

physical_center_3classrooms.note: Three classrooms, two instructors. Scale-up model.

churn.tawjihi_note: Tawjihi centers lose all students in June-July after exams. Enrollment resets fully each September. Plan for 0 revenue June-August.

breakEven_months.physical_center_academic_year_note: For seasonal businesses like Tawjihi centers, break-even should be measured in academic months (September-May = 9 months), not calendar months. 7 calendar months of break-even means the business may never cover summer fixed costs.

tawjihi_center_realistic.note: Measured in academic months active. Summer gap of 3 months adds ~4500-6000 JOD to effective break-even cost.

commonRisks: Tawjihi centers earn nothing June-August — 3 months of fixed rent and salaries with zero revenue
Negative perception of vocational training vs university degrees
Seasonal lumpiness: back-to-school spike September, Tawjihi season April-June, summer crash June-August
Teacher poaching: competing centers and private clients target your best teachers during peak Tawjihi season — sign 12-month contracts before September
Low Tawjihi seasons create enrollment crashes — re-enrollment rate below 25% is a red flag

redFlags.summerGapReserve_JOD: Must hold 3 months of fixed costs as reserve before June — minimum 4000-8000 JOD depending on model

jordanContext.summerDead: June-August: near-zero enrollment — must have cash reserve to survive
jordanContext.acquisitionChannel: WhatsApp groups + word of mouth — parents share tutor recommendations
jordanContext.onlineGrowth: Post-COVID online tutoring normalized. Hybrid (physical + online materials) now expected.
